verdict:
NEGATIVE.  The 600-cell Dirac spectrum does not naturally mimic the SM 1-loop correction to the Higgs mass.

Key findings:
  1. Hard-cutoff CW with Λ = 10¹⁶ GeV gives m_H ≈ 1420 GeV — unphysically large because the 600-cell contains ~240 eigenvalues with O(1) Yukawas, whereas the SM has only one heavy fermion (top).
  2. Using a physical matching scale μ = v gives m_H ≈ 174.7 GeV — still 354.3σ from PDG.
  3. The best-fit Yukawa scale c ≈ 0.196 is ~24% from the natural scale c ≈ 0.158 (which maps the largest eigenvalue to the top Yukawa).
  4. The σ-field mechanism (Connes–Marcolli) remains the only known way to reach 125 GeV in NCG, and Wave 5.3 proved it unavailable in H₄.
